reasoning: The 'unsafe' verdict stemmed from a false positive in an example config file. The flagged command substitution in kqemu.example is inert during package build/install and poses no risk to the user building this package. All executable components (PKGBUILD and main script) are safe.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged an example config file (kqemu.example) as containing command injection risk, while Deepseek correctly recognized it as non-executable documentation.
coverage_issues: Qwen missed Git metadata files (config, description, packed-refs) though they're low-risk
Both models prioritized example configs over higher-value build scripts
false_positives: kqemu.example: Example config with command substitution isn't executed during makepkg (Qwen)
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Focus PKGBUILD, install hooks, and executable scripts first before examples/docs
Clarify threat model in prompt: Non-executed configs aren't risks unless used in build functions
Reduce false positives: Train models to distinguish between active build components and inert examples
Optimize coverage: Skip Git metadata unless referenced in build scripts
